headline: Parts of Luzon to have power interruptions on May 18-19  GMA News Online
text:
Meralco has announced upcoming power interruptions over the weekend in provincial areas outside of Metro Manila.
On the Meralco website, the power distributor said service interruptions would be experienced in the following areas between 11:30 p.m. and 11:59 p.m. on Saturday, May 18, and between 7 a.m. and 7:30 a.m. on Sunday, May 19, due to maintenance works inside the Meralco – Tayabas Substation:
Meanwhile, the following areas will also experience interruptions from 5:30 a.m. to 6 a.m. and between 6:00 p.m. and 6:30 p.m. on Saturday resulting from NGCP preventive maintenance and testing works inside the NGCP – Dasmariñas Substation:
Meanwhile, power interruptions will be experienced between 2 a.m. and 7 a.m. on Sunday due to line reconstruction works along Pan-Philippine Highway:
— Jiselle Anne Casucian/ VDV, GMA Integrated News

headline: PAGASA 40 areas under danger level heat index on May 17  GMA News Online
text:
Forty areas may experience a heat index at the danger level on Friday, May 17, the state weather bureau PAGASA said.According to PAGASA's heat index forecast issued at 5 p.m. Thursday,, the following areas predicted to have heat index ranging from 42°C to 46°C are:
42°C
43°C
44°C
45°C
46°C
Baguio City, Benguet remains to have the lowest predicted heat index at 25°C.According to PAGASA, areas with heat index ranging from 42°C to 51°C are categorized under the danger level.Heat index is the measure of the temperature that a person feels, which is different from the actual air temperature. It is computed by factoring in the humidity and the air temperature.PAGASA said heat cramps and heat exhaustion are likely in areas experiencing a danger level heat index, which is from 42 to 51°C. Heat stroke is also possible with continued exposure.The public was advised to stay hydrated, limit outdoor activity, and wear fresh clothes to avoid heat-related illnesses.—Jiselle Casucian/RF. GMA Integrated

headline: Century Properties net income up 35% in Q1  GMA News Online
text:
Antonio-led real estate developer Century Properties Group Inc. on Thursday reported a 35% increase in its first-quarter bottom line, which it attributed to revenue growth buoyed by its affordable residential unit.
In a regulatory filing, Century Properties said its consolidated net income after tax (NIAT) climbed to P410 million in the first quarter from P302 million the same period in 2023, as revenues increased by 7% to P3.6 billion from P3.3 billion.
Its First-Home Residential Development Platform (PHirst) reported P2.1 billion in revenues or 58% of the total, while its in-city vertical developments contributed 34% or P1.1 billion, and its commercial leasing segment 9% or P314 million. Its property management segment contributed 3% or P99 million.
“The affordable residential market’s sentiment for quality homes defied the odds of elevated interest and stubborn inflation rates as shown by our strong first-quarter performance,” Century Properties chief finance officer Ponciano Carreon Jr. said.
“Sales take-up of our PHirst home products remains strong and we see this further improving for the rest of the year. We are bullish on this resilient segment of the industry without losing sight of the niche market for luxury homes,” he added.
Century Pacific counts as subsidiaries Century Communities Corp., Century City Development Corp., Century Limitless Corp., Century Properties Management Inc., PHirst Park Homes Inc., Century Destinations and Lifestyle Corp., and Century Nuliv Development Corp.
Shares in the company were last traded at P0.295 per share, up by P0.005 or 1.72% from Wednesday’s finish of P0.290. — BM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Parts of Metro Manila to experience power interruptions on May 18  GMA News Online
text:
Meralco has announced that several areas in Metro Manila will be experiencing power interruptions over the weekend.
On the Meralco website, the power company said that maintenance works will be causing the interruptions in said areas.
Parts of Quezon City (Diliman) will be having interruptions between 9 a.m. and 2 p.m. on Saturday due to line reconductoring works along Masaya and Maginhawa Streets in Barangays UP Village and San Vicente in Diliman, Quezon City.Areas affected in the vicinity are:
Interruptions between 11 a.m. and 11:30 a.m. on Saturday will also occur in the following areas of Caloocan and Malabon City due to line reconstruction works along Prelaya Street in Barangay Tugatog in Malabon:

headline: Shear line, easterlies continue to affect PH  GMA News Online
text:
The shear line and the easterlies will continue to bring cloudy skies and rain showers to several parts of the country.
According to PAGASA’s daily weather forecast released on Thursday afternoon, Batanes, Cagayan, and Isabela will have cloudy skies with scattered rain showers and thunderstorms caused by the shear line.
Partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rain showers or thunderstorms are expected in the rest of the country due to the easterlies.
Flash floods or landslides may occur due to these weather systems.
Meanwhile, the northern and western sections of Northern Luzon will have moderate to strong winds with moderate to rough seas.
The rest of the country will experience light to moderate winds with slight to moderate coastal waters.
Sunrise in Metro Manila will be at 5:28 a.m. on Friday. —Jiselle Anne Casucian/VBL, GMA Integrated News

headline: House panel to probe killings in Duterte war on drugs  GMA News Online
text:
The House human rights panel will investigate the drug war deaths during the administration of former President Rodrigo Duterte on May 22, House human rights panel chairman Bienvenido Abante said Thursday. 
Abante said the inquiry, which will be attended by the relatives of drug war victims, police officers, and Cabinet officials from the Duterte administration, remains significant and timely in the interest of justice. 
“Ang tanong siguro ng iba, why are we conducting an inquiry into the deaths of alleged drug addicts or drug dealers? First, we must begin with the principle that each life is valuable and that each life lost is a profound tragedy,” Abante said in a press conference.
“Secondly, we must point out that those who lost their lives, like every Filipino, are protected by the rights granted by the Constitution, one of which is due process,” Abante added.
The nation, Abante said, owes the victims of drug war deaths justice. 
“They were not convicted, they were silenced. They were denied their rights. It is our responsibility to the victims and their families to seek the truth,” Abante said. 
Abante then said that his committee has the mandate tl diligently look into the accusations of human rights abuses amid the anti-drug war police operations.
Based on government records, around 6,200 drug suspects were killed during the Duterte administration’s anti-drug police operations.
Human rights organizations, however, argue that this number goes as high as 30,000 due to unreported drug-related killings.
Former President Duterte is under International Criminal Court probe over alleged crimes against humanity committed during his Presidency over this bloody drug war policy during his time as Chief Executive. —NB, GMA Integrated News
